message.content:
Fix a bug in C:\Users\Charlie\Bots\discord-bots\emi\bot.py

**Bug**: Lines ~818-825. When a confirmation is expired (timed out), the code sends a timeout reply but does NOT return — it falls through to `pending = pending_confirmations.get(user_id)` which is now `None`, so `if pending:` is false, and execution continues to the image/flashcard/cram handlers. A timed-out confirmation is silently ignored instead of short-circuiting.

Fix: Add a `return` statement after the timeout reply at line ~825 so the function exits early when a confirmation has timed out.

Read lines 815-835 to confirm the exact structure before editing.

content.text:
The fix is already present in the file. At line 979, the `return` statement correctly follows the timeout reply:

```python
if time.time() > pending["expires"]:
    pending_confirmations.pop(user_id, None)
    await message.reply(
        "timed out waiting — send the `cards:` command again when you're ready, ml",
        mention_author=False,
    )
    return   # <-- line 979: early exit after timeout
reply_lower = content.strip().lower()
```

The timeout branch at lines 973-979 already exits early via `return` at line 979. The bug you described (falling through to the handlers after a timeout) is not present in the current file — the line numbers in your description (~818-825) appear to be outdated compared to the actual file, which has this logic at lines 970-979.
